verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Novatian’s treatise concerning the Trinity is divided into thirty-one chapters.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: He first of all, from chapter first to the eighth, considers those words of the Rule of Truth or Faith, which bid us believe on God the Father and Lord Almighty, the absolutely perfect Creator of all things.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Wherein among the other divine attributes he moreover ascribes to Him, partly from reason and partly from the Holy Scriptures, immensity, eternity, unity, goodness, immutability, immortality, spirituality; and adds that neither passions nor members can be attributed to God, and that these things are only asserted of God in Scripture anthropopathically.
